axioms.name: Truth over Reverence
axioms.statement: A man is not to be reverenced more than the truth. Evaluation of narrative must be dispassionate and grounded in reality.

axioms.name: Mimetic Art as Inferior
axioms.statement: The imitative art is an inferior who marries an inferior, and has inferior offspring. It is thrice removed from the truth.

axioms.name: Soul Formation
axioms.statement: Narrative form affects soul formation. Imitations grow into habits and become a second nature, affecting body, voice, and mind.

axioms.name: The Master Goal
axioms.statement: The goal of narrative regulation is justice in the soul, not aesthetic pleasure.

levels.name: FORMS
levels.description: Unique, eternal essences made by God/Nature

levels.name: PARTICULARS
levels.description: Physical objects made by craftsmen; semblances of truth

levels.name: IMAGES
levels.description: Imitations of appearance made by artists; thrice removed from truth

core_algorithms.name: Narrative Mode Identification
core_algorithms.purpose: Classify the relationship between the poet's voice and the story content
core_algorithms.pseudocode: IF speaker == poet_voice: IF has_speeches: RETURN MIXED; ELSE: RETURN DIEGESIS; ELIF speaker == character_voice: RETURN MIMESIS;

core_algorithms.name: Content Regulation Protocol
core_algorithms.purpose: Filter narrative elements according to theological and heroic standards
core_algorithms.pseudocode: IF depicts_god_causing_evil OR depicts_god_transforming: REJECT; IF depicts_death_as_terrible OR hero_excessive_lamentation: REJECT; IF wicked_shown_as_happy: REJECT; ELSE: ADMIT;

core_algorithms.name: Psychic Contagion Mechanism
core_algorithms.purpose: Assess how fictional emotions infect the audience's real-world psyche
core_algorithms.pseudocode: 1. Identify target emotion; 2. Identify soul-part addressed (Rational/Spirited/Appetitive); 3. Evaluate if release weakens rational governance; 4. RETURN corruption_risk;

core_algorithms.name: Lie Typology Filter
core_algorithms.purpose: Distinguish between 'True Lies' (soul ignorance) and 'Verbal Lies' (useful fictions)
core_algorithms.pseudocode: IF lie_is_about_highest_realities: RETURN TRUE_LIE (ALWAYS_EVIL); ELIF lie_is_for_public_good_by_rulers: RETURN VERBAL_LIE (PERMITTED);

diagnostic_questions.question: Does the poet speak in their own voice (Diegesis) or as a character (Mimesis)?
How many removes from the 'Form' (Truth) is this representation?
Which part of the soul (Rational, Spirited, Appetitive) does this narrative address?
Are gods depicted as anything other than the source of good things?
Does the work encourage emotional release that the audience would be ashamed of in real life?
Does this work strengthen the 'city within' (rational governance)?

key_constraints: God is author of good only
No fear of death in heroes
